lesson: When one barrel of a multihead system cools fine but another stays warm while the compressor runs, the liquid-line refrigerant solenoid valve for that barrel is failing to open. Replacing the compressor is an expensive mistake.
symptoms: Left barrel is frozen solid, but right barrel is warm and soupy.
Left barrel display shows 18°F (-8°C), right barrel shows 45°F (7°C).

tests.temp-probe: Probe agrees with the panel: one barrel sitting warm in the mid-40s °F, the others in spec.
tests.inspect-beater: One barrel's product sits warm and liquid while its neighbours hold texture. Nothing binding in any of the beaters.

flavour: Solenoid was stuck on 'no flow'. Now the cold is back in business.
authenticityNote: Multi-barrel machines share a single compressor but use separate solenoid valves on the liquid lines to control cooling to each evaporator barrel independently. If a solenoid valve fails closed mechanically, that barrel gets no refrigeration.
